title: SACRAMENTO, CA

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 865 establishes the Dual Language Immersion Education Instructional Materials Grant Program.",
    "The program has a one-time appropriation of $5 million to increase the availability of instructional materials in Dual Language Immersion programs across California.",
    "Over 70% of the district represented by Assemblymember González speaks a language other than English at home.",
    "Dual language immersion is an effective educational model that supports increased engagement among students.",
    "Increasing resources and opportunities for dual language immersion programs has been shown to improve academic outcomes for students.",
    "Dual language immersion programs help strengthen students' foundations for future success.",
    "Dual language immersion is important for increasing multilingualism in schools.",
    "The grant will support teachers in dual immersion programs by funding the development and purchase of standards-aligned instructional materials.",
    "Three of the top five languages among English learner students in California are Asian languages: Mandarin, Vietnamese, and Cantonese.",
    "Dual immersion programs have shown successful results for students from both non-native and native English-speaking backgrounds in gaining proficiency in multiple languages.",
    "$3.5 million will be allocated to DLI programs in the five most common partner languages among English learner students: Spanish, Vietnamese, Mandarin, Arabic, and Cantonese.",
    "$1.5 million will be allocated to DLI programs in other partner languages, such as Korean, Armenian, and Japanese.",
    "These grants will allow schools to develop or purchase standards-aligned instructional materials in partner languages."
  ]
}
```
